rationale: Putin's public admission that Ukraine attacks are hurting Russia's economy and society is a new qualitative data point for Poland's security calculus, though no battlefield change occurred.

top_events.title: Putin admits Ukraine attacks hitting Russian economy and society
top_events.summary: Putin's rare public admission of economic and social strain from the war is a new signal for Poland — it may indicate weakening Russian capacity, but also potential for unpredictable escalation near Poland's border.

top_events.title: Iran deal 'never been closer' — US Congressman warns against money flows
top_events.summary: Progress on an Iran deal could ease global energy prices, offering indirect relief to Polish households still sensitive to energy costs, though no timeline is confirmed.

top_events.title: China arrests US academic for espionage
top_events.summary: Rising US-China tensions over espionage cases add background noise to global geopolitical instability, with marginal indirect relevance to Poland's security environment.
